entries.label: TypeScript
entries.description: Strongly typed superset of JavaScript. Default choice for all new projects. #1 language on GitHub by contributor count as of 2025.

entries.label: React
entries.description: Preferred UI library. React 19 with Server Components and the App Router is the default for all new work.

entries.label: Next.js
entries.description: Full-stack React framework. App Router is the current standard; Pages Router is now legacy.

entries.label: Python
entries.description: Go-to language for AI/ML scripting, agent backends, and data pipelines.

entries.label: Tailwind CSS
entries.description: Utility-first CSS framework. Tailwind v4 with CSS-first config adopted across all projects.

entries.label: FastAPI
entries.description: De facto standard for Python API services. Native async, automatic OpenAPI generation, and ideal for AI/agent backends. Stack Overflow 2025 showed the largest single-year adoption gain of any Python framework.

entries.label: Rust
entries.description: Evaluating for systems-level WebAssembly targets and performance-critical tooling.

entries.label: Astro
entries.description: Islands architecture for content-heavy sites. Achieved highest Lighthouse performance scores in 2025 benchmarks. Strong alternative to Next.js for marketing and documentation sites.

entries.label: Svelte 5 / SvelteKit
entries.description: Compile-time reactivity with the Svelte 5 runes system. 180% growth in 2025. No runtime overhead. Evaluating for projects where bundle size and performance are critical.

entries.label: GraphQL
entries.description: Considered on a per-project basis when flexible querying is needed.

entries.label: Next.js Pages Router
entries.description: The App Router is the current standard. New projects must not use the Pages Router — it accumulates architectural debt that compounds. Existing projects should plan migration.

entries.label: AWS
entries.description: Primary cloud. GovCloud experience including first GreenGrass/IoT Core GovCloud deployment.

entries.label: Docker
entries.description: Standard container runtime for all service packaging and deployment.

entries.label: Kubernetes
entries.description: Container orchestration for production workloads.

entries.label: GitHub Actions
entries.description: CI/CD of choice for open source and personal projects.

entries.label: Vercel
entries.description: Preferred deployment platform for Next.js projects.

entries.label: Model Context Protocol
entries.description: Anthropic's open standard for connecting AI agents to tools and data sources. ThoughtWorks Vol.33 called it the dominant theme of 2025. 97M+ monthly SDK downloads. Adopted by OpenAI, Google, Microsoft. Donated to Linux Foundation.

entries.label: ClickHouse
entries.description: Open-source columnar OLAP database with incremental materialized views. ThoughtWorks Vol.33 Adopt ring. Preferred for real-time developer-facing analytics over legacy data warehouse stacks.

entries.label: Arm Cloud Compute
entries.description: AWS Graviton, Azure Cobalt, GCP Axion. ThoughtWorks Vol.33 Adopt for cost and energy efficiency. Default choice for new CI/CD and containerized workloads to reduce cloud spend.

entries.label: Edge Computing
entries.description: Cloudflare Workers, Durable Objects, and AWS Lambda@Edge are now mainstream. The three-tier hybrid architecture (cloud + on-premises + edge) is the standard for AI and latency-sensitive workloads.

entries.label: Vector Databases
entries.description: Critical infrastructure for agent memory and RAG retrieval. Evaluating pgvector (Postgres-native), Qdrant, and Pinecone. Bain identifies vector stores as foundational for all agentic AI architectures.

entries.label: Azure
entries.description: Used on select enterprise Microsoft-stack engagements.

entries.label: Deno 2
entries.description: Node.js-compatible runtime with full npm support, built-in TypeScript, and secure-by-default permissions. Deno 2 removed the last major adoption blockers. Evaluating for new server-side TypeScript services.

entries.label: Quantum Computing
entries.description: Monitoring IBM Q, AWS Braket, and Google Willow. Near-term priority is post-quantum cryptography migration — NIST 2024 standards (lattice-based algorithms) are finalized. General business applications remain 5-10 years out.

entries.label: Blockchain
entries.description: Enterprise ledger use cases proven at Lockheed Martin. All major 2025-2026 consulting firm reports (Deloitte, McKinsey, Accenture, Bain) have deprioritized new enterprise blockchain investments — use cases remain narrow and alternatives have matured.

entries.label: Vite
entries.description: Build tool of choice for SPAs and libraries. Replaced CRA entirely.

entries.label: Vitest
entries.description: Fast unit test runner that pairs naturally with Vite.

entries.label: Playwright
entries.description: End-to-end browser testing. Replaced Cypress for all new test suites.

entries.label: Claude Code
entries.description: Agentic AI coding assistant. Integrated into daily engineering workflow.

entries.label: OpenTelemetry
entries.description: De facto standard for vendor-neutral observability instrumentation. CNCF graduated project. Decouples telemetry generation from analysis backends (Datadog, Grafana, Honeycomb). Required in all new service builds.

entries.label: Cursor
entries.description: AI-native code editor with deep codebase context awareness and multi-file refactoring. ThoughtWorks Vol.32 Trial, now firmly Adopt. Primary development environment for complex, multi-file work.

entries.label: Terraform
entries.description: IaC standard for multi-cloud provisioning.

entries.label: Langfuse
entries.description: Open-source LLM observability platform. Tracing, prompt versioning, evaluation, and playground. Self-hostable, OpenTelemetry-compatible alternative to LangSmith. 19K+ GitHub stars.

entries.label: Windsurf
entries.description: Agentic AI IDE with the Cascade engine. Evaluating alongside Cursor for autonomous multi-step coding tasks. ThoughtWorks Vol.32 Trial.

entries.label: Storybook
entries.description: Useful for component-heavy design systems; overhead is high for smaller projects.

entries.label: Generative AI Assistants
entries.description: Claude, GPT-4o, and Gemini integrated into daily engineering workflows.

entries.label: RAG Pipelines
entries.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is the standard architecture for knowledge-grounded LLM apps.

entries.label: Zero Trust Architecture
entries.description: Perimeter-less security model applied across all infrastructure design. Cross-firm consulting consensus as baseline requirement for 2025-2026.

entries.label: Threat Modeling
entries.description: STRIDE-based modeling baked into sprint ceremonies. Now extended to cover AI agent threat surfaces.

entries.label: Trunk-Based Dev
entries.description: Short-lived branches with frequent integration to main.

entries.label: Context Engineering
entries.description: Systematic management of what an AI agent knows at each step in a workflow. Covers RAG, memory strategies, tool selection, and session state design. ThoughtWorks Vol.33 identifies this as the 2025 successor to prompt engineering.

entries.label: Agentic AI Workflows
entries.description: Multi-step autonomous agent pipelines with tool-use. Frameworks: LangGraph, Claude Agent SDK, OpenAI Agents SDK. MCP is the integration protocol. All major consulting firms cite this as the #1 enterprise technology investment for 2025-2026. Only 11% of orgs have it in production.

entries.label: Small Language Models
entries.description: Task-specific SLMs preferred over large general-purpose models for agent subtasks. Deloitte and Bain recommend microservices-style agent architecture: one SLM per discrete function, orchestrated by a manager agent.

entries.label: Supply Chain Security
entries.description: SLSA attestation and SBOM (SPDX/CycloneDX formats) moving from compliance checkbox to active engineering practice. CISA mandate updates and increasing open-source dependency attacks are driving urgency in 2025.

entries.label: Post-Quantum Cryptography
entries.description: NIST August 2024 PQC standards (lattice-based CRYSTALS-Kyber and hash-based SPHINCS+) are finalized. Deloitte, McKinsey, and Bain all recommend beginning migration roadmap now. Apple, Google, and Microsoft are already integrating. Build cryptographic agility into all new systems.

entries.label: Micro-Frontends
entries.description: Module Federation evaluated for large team codebases.

entries.label: Design Tokens
entries.description: CSS custom properties as the single source of truth for design systems.

entries.label: WebAssembly / WASI
entries.description: WASI 0.2 and the Component Model shipped with async support. Sub-millisecond cold starts, kilobyte-sized binaries. Cloudflare and Fastly process billions of requests via Wasm. A genuine container alternative for edge and serverless specialists.

entries.label: Digital Twins
entries.description: Virtual replicas of physical systems for simulation and operational optimization. Accenture, McKinsey, and Bain all highlight supply chain and infrastructure applications. Evaluating for IoT and enterprise asset management use cases.

entries.label: Physical AI / Humanoid Robots
entries.description: Foundation models embedded in robots for generalist physical capabilities. ~$2.5B VC investment in 2024. All four major consulting firms tracking (Deloitte, McKinsey, Accenture, Bain). 2-5 years from meaningful scaled deployment — monitor now.

entries.label: Ungoverned AI Agents
entries.description: Deploying autonomous agents without behavioral observability, rate limiting, and access controls is high risk. Prompt injection, cross-tool data exfiltration, and privilege escalation are active attack vectors. Apply API-level security rigor to every agent tool and MCP server endpoint.
